# Project Progress

## Current Stage
prover  (iteration 012 — Path-2 Čech scaffold sub-step. Iter-011 closed the **first protected sorry** (`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`) cleanly: sorry count 10 → 9, kernel-only axioms, signature preserved (only the user-authorised `noncomputable` modifier was added). Iter-012 opens Path-2 of Phase A step 6 (Čech cohomology of the structure sheaf, the multi-iteration route to Serre finiteness). Iter-012 plan-agent has issued `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for two new declarations in `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` (`Scheme.cechCochain_OC`, `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`); the iter-012 refactor agent will execute, then this plan agent will be re-invoked for post-refactor verification + prover objectives.)

## Stages
- [x] init
- [x] autoformalize
- [ ] prover
- [ ] polish

## Big-picture context

This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.

`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). Iter-005/006/007 closed Phase A steps 1–5 + polish. Iter-008 closed Track 2 (étale sheafification of `PicardFunctorAb`). Iter-009 closed the Phase A step 5/6 bridge (`HModule` infrastructure for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology). Iter-010 closed the Phase A step 6 *algebraic preliminary* (`HModule_zero_linearEquiv`). Iter-011 closed the **first protected sorry** (`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`). **Iter-012 opens Path-2 Čech scaffold (Phase A step 6 *proper* sub-step)** toward the multi-iteration Serre finiteness theorem that downstream `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` will eventually consume.

## Iteration 011 prover-round verification (this pass)

The iter-011 prover round closed `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` with the probe-confirmed body `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`. Plan-agent verification (this iter-012 pass) confirms:

- **Sorry count**: `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` returns `9 total across 3 file(s)`. Distribution: 5 `Jacobian.lean`, 3 `AbelJacobi.lean`, 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`. Pre-prover: 10 (refactor + sorry); post-prover: 9. Matches expectation exactly.
- **Compilation**: `lean_diagnostic_messages AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` (zero warnings, zero errors). The previous `declaration uses 'sorry'` warning at L65 col 19 has cleared.
- **Axioms**: `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.genus` returns `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}` — kernel-only, exactly the standard triple. No `sorryAx`. No new `axiom` declarations anywhere.
- **Body**: term-mode one-liner `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`, exactly as probe-confirmed; no name adjustments forced (the file's `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` + `open CategoryTheory Limits` resolve `Scheme.HModule` and `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` correctly).
- **Protected signature**: preserved verbatim — `noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ`. Argument names, types, order, and return type frozen. Only the user-authorised `noncomputable` modifier was added (by the iter-011 refactor); `archon-protected.yaml` unchanged.
- **First protected closure of the project.** Migrated to `task_done.md` this pass. 100% first-edit closure rate; streak now six consecutive prover rounds.

The iter-011 prover round is therefore accepted. Iter-012 work proceeds.

## Iteration 012 plan: Path-2 Čech scaffold (Phase A step 6 *proper* sub-step)

**Status: REFACTOR DIRECTIVE PENDING.** Iter-012 plan-agent has written `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` directing the refactor agent to scaffold two new declarations in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`:

1. `noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC ... : CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ := sorry` — the Čech cochain complex of `toModuleKSheaf C` for an arbitrary indexed open cover `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`. Closure body (iter-012 prover): `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))`.
2. `noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC ... : ModuleCat.{u} k := sorry` — its `n`-th cohomology. Closure body (iter-012 prover): `(Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n`.

Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirms both bodies typecheck end-to-end against the post-iter-011 project state, kernel-only axioms. Required typeclass instances (`HasProducts (ModuleCat.{u} k)`, `HasFiniteProducts (Opens X)`, `Preadditive (ModuleCat.{u} k)`) all auto-resolved.

**Mathematical content.** The Čech complex of the structure sheaf for a finite open cover is the standard combinatorial object computing $H^i$ via `cechComplexFunctor`; on an *acyclic* cover it agrees with derived-functor cohomology. For Phase A step 6 *proper* (Serre finiteness on a proper $k$-curve) the route is: (i) fix an affine cover (Mathlib has `Scheme.affineCover`); (ii) prove that an affine cover is acyclic for quasi-coherent sheaves (Serre's vanishing on affines, multi-iteration); (iii) apply the comparison theorem to identify `cechCohomology_OC` with `HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`; (iv) use finiteness of $\Gamma(\text{affine open}, \mathcal O_C)$ over $k$ on a proper curve plus dimension vanishing in degree $> 1$ to conclude `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) i)`. Iter-012 lays only step (i)'s carrier; iter-013+ takes on (ii)–(iv).

**Why iter-012 is a single iteration.** Both `cechCochain_OC` and `cechCohomology_OC` are *carrying-only* declarations — direct applications of Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor` and `HomologicalComplex.homology` to the iter-006 `toModuleKSheaf C`. No new mathematical content; the carriers exist purely to expose stable API surfaces that iter-013+ will operate on. Both probe-confirmed at one-line bodies. The "scaffold + close in one iteration" pattern matches iter-009 (`HModule`) and iter-010 (`HModule_zero_linearEquiv`).

**Blueprint.** `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex` has been extended this pass with a new section "Čech cohomology of the structure sheaf (iter-012 scaffold)" containing `def:Scheme_cechCochain_OC` and `def:Scheme_cechCohomology_OC` blocks. The prover does not need to update the blueprint; the review agent will add `\leanok` markers post-prover-round.

## Current Objectives

This iteration is in its **refactor phase**: the dispatcher will run the refactor agent next, executing `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`. The plan agent will be re-invoked for post-refactor verification + prover objectives. There are therefore no `## Current Objectives` for the prover this pass — they will be set in the post-refactor plan-agent pass.

- **Sorry count**: 9 → 11 (= 9 + 2 new scaffold sorries on `cechCochain_OC` and `cechCohomology_OC`).
- **Compilation**: `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` should return exactly two `declaration uses 'sorry'` warnings (one per new declaration), zero errors. `Genus.lean` should remain `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` (the iter-011 closure must remain intact). All other files unchanged.
- **Protected signatures**: unchanged. `archon-protected.yaml` unchanged.
- **Imports**: no new imports added or removed (the existing `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb`, which transitively brings in Mathlib, is sufficient — `cechComplexFunctor` and `sheafToPresheaf` resolve through it).

## Post-prover anticipated state (after iter-012 prover round)

- **Compilation**: `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` should return `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`.
- **Axioms**: `lean_verify Scheme.cechCochain_OC` and `lean_verify Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` return only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.
- **Blueprint**: `\leanok` markers on the statement and proof of `def:Scheme_cechCochain_OC` and `def:Scheme_cechCohomology_OC` (added by the iter-012 review agent).

## Off-limits this iteration

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` — closed iter-011; do not edit.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` (5 protected sorries) — gated on Phase C representability + Albanese, multi-iteration. The user's `noncomputable` authorisation extends here but is not actionable yet.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` (3 protected sorries) — gated on `Jacobian C` first, multi-iteration.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` — `PicardFunctor.representable` (L185) is documented dead-end deferral until `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` lands in Mathlib + the FGA argument is built.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean`, `Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean`, `Picard/LineBundle.lean`, `Picard/FunctorAb.lean`, `Rigidity.lean` — closed in earlier iterations; nothing to do this round.

## Known dead ends (do not retry)

- The 8 currently-blocked protected sorries (`Jacobian.lean` × 5, `AbelJacobi.lean` × 3) — gated on Phase C representability + Phase E geometric content + multi-iteration upstream Mathlib advances. Do not assign as direct prover objectives. The user's `noncomputable` authorisation removes the modifier-level gate but the upstream content gates remain.
- `PicardFunctor.representable` (`Picard/Functor.lean` L185) — closing on the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle` is a forbidden shortcut. Honest closure requires `LineBundle` refinement (gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`, still absent in current Mathlib) plus the FGA argument. Multi-iteration; do not retry.

- Vacuous-functor closures (`Discrete PUnit`, etc.) — destroy the downstream Picard chain; rejected on sight.
- Vacuous-sheaf closures (`PUnit.{u+1}`-valued, etc.) — destroy the downstream cohomology chain; rejected on sight.

- For iter-012: scaffolding a new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/Cech.lean` instead of extending `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` — directive is explicit on this.
- New `axiom` declarations — forbidden by plan-agent rules.
- Symmetric topological-equality form of rigidity (`∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x ⇒ g₁ = g₂`) — false in characteristic `p` (Frobenius). Resolved iter-002.
- Naive `AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor` (without explicit universe annotations) — does not unify under `u+1 =?= max u v`; explicit `.{u+1, u}` annotation required (resolved iter-008).
- `HModule` as `def` instead of `noncomputable abbrev` — instance synthesis fails on the wrapper (probe-verified iter-009).
- `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` with `≃` (`homEquiv₀`) or `≃+` (`addEquiv₀`) instead of `≃ₗ[k]` (`linearEquiv₀`) — the chosen signature is `≃ₗ[k]`; downstream `Module.Finite k` transport requires the `k`-linear refinement (resolved iter-010).
- Polish-shape `HModule_forget` (`forget₂ HModule ↔ Sheaf.H ((sheafCompose _ (forget₂)).obj F)`) — Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` uses `AddCommGrpCat.of (ULift ℤ)` as the unit, whereas `HModule` uses `ModuleCat.of k k`; the comparison is not a one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent finding, supersedes session-15 `recommendations.md`).
- Track 1 / Phase A step 6 *proper* (Serre finiteness on a proper curve) — multi-iteration; iter-012 lays only the Čech-side carrier (`cechCohomology_OC`), not the comparison or finiteness theorems.
